creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why

content: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.
contentSnippet: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
author: Jay Peters
content:
![Chat GPT logo on a graphic green background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/STK155_OPEN_AI_4_CVirginia_D.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Last month, [OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt), and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities as part of an effort to avoid "side quests," and Peebles' departure is just one of [many recent changes](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/908513/the-vibes-are-off-at-openai) as the company moves to focus more on coding and enterprise use.

As part of a note Peebles posted on X, [he said](https://x.com/billpeeb/status/2045225014807670949?s=20):

> I am immensely grateful to Sam, Mark, Aditya and Jakub for fostering a research environment that allowed us to pursue ideas off-the-beaten path from the company's mainline roadmap. It's tempting in life to mode collapse to the most i …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/914463/openai-sora-bill-peebles-kevin-weil-leaving-departing)
contentSnippet:
Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities as part of an effort to avoid "side quests," and Peebles' departure is just one of many recent changes as the company moves to focus more on coding and enterprise use.
As part of a note Peebles posted on X, he said:
I am immensely grateful to Sam, Mark, Aditya and Jakub for fostering a research environment that allowed us to pursue ideas off-the-beaten path from the company's mainline roadmap. It's tempting in life to mode collapse to the most i …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities as part of an effort to avoid "side quests," and Peebles' departure is just one of many recent changes as the company […]

title: (No AI) Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A screenshot of World ID verification on Tinder](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/world-id-tinder-verify.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Tinder users who prove they're a real person by [visiting an identity-verifying orb](https://world.org/blog/announcements/online-dating-gaming-event-tickets-proof-of-human-everyday-life) will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb. World, which was co-founded by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, initially tested Tinder verification using [its facial scanning orbs](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/18/24273691/world-orb-sam-altman-iris-scan-crypto-token) through a pilot program in Japan last year. It's now expanding the service to "select markets, including Japan and the United States."

To verify that they're not a bot or an AI agent, users have to physically visit one of World's orbs in person. [According to World](https://world.org/orb), the orb "takes pictures of your face and eyes, then encrypts and sto …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/914385/world-id-tinder-identity-verifying-orb)

Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb. World, which was co-founded by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, initially tested Tinder verification using its facial scanning orbs through a pilot program in Japan last year. It's now expanding the service to "select markets, including Japan and the United States." 
To verify that they're not a bot or an AI agent, users have to physically visit one of World's orbs in person. According to World, the orb "takes pictures of your face and eyes, then encrypts and sto …
Read the full story at The Verge.
